adventure.explanation: Remote Yorkshire Dales with iconic passes and wild moorland create pure cycling adventure.

altitude.explanation: High pubs and moorland summits, but not extreme altitude sickness territory.

climate.explanation: Classic British summer - sun, rain, and wind all possible in one day.

community.explanation: Strong charity ethos with Dave Rayner Fund support builds tight-knit cycling community.

elevation.explanation: 10,500ft over 106 miles - proper British climbing sufferfest.

expenses.explanation: £50 entry represents excellent value for this calibre of event.

experience.explanation: Iconic climbs through cinematic Yorkshire scenery - bucket list ride.

field_depth.explanation: 500 riders but sportive format limits elite depth.

length.explanation: 106 miles of continuous Dales punishment - full day commitment.

logistics.explanation: New Settle HQ with parking and facilities simplifies access.

race_quality.explanation: Perfectly organised with feed stations and route tweaks for accessibility.

technicality.explanation: Steep ramps to 18% plus twisty descents demand bike handling skills.

value.explanation: Top-tier scenery and challenge for modest entry fee.

cultural_impact.explanation: Alternative to Fred Whitton with strong Yorkshire cycling heritage.

prestige.explanation: Regional Yorkshire sportive running 20+ years, well-known within UK cycling but limited international profile.

course_description.character: Brutal Yorkshire Dales epic featuring seven monster climbs through wild moorland, remote valleys, and iconic high passes. Cinematic scenery meets relentless gradient.

suffering_zones.label: Langcliffe Scar
suffering_zones.description: 2.3km @ 7.8% avg (18% max) - brutal race opener after 3km warm-up

suffering_zones.label: Fleet Moss
suffering_zones.description: Highest road in Yorkshire - bleak moorland exposure

suffering_zones.label: Tan Hill Inn
suffering_zones.description: Highest pub in England - remote grit test

suffering_zones.label: Buttertubs Pass
suffering_zones.description: Iconic Tour de Yorkshire climb with butter churn landmarks

suffering_zones.label: Coal Road
suffering_zones.description: 2.8km @ 7.8% avg to highest mainline station in England

course_description.signature_challenge: Coal Road to Dent Station - relentless 7.8% gradient delivering you to England's highest mainline railway station through remote gritstone wilderness.

logistics.overview: New HQ at Settle with parking, changing rooms, post-ride food and beer. Multiple feed stations including Moorcock Inn.
logistics.accommodation: Settle offers B&Bs, pubs, and camping. Grassington (15min drive) and Skipton (20min) provide more options.
logistics.getting_there: Settle train station direct from Leeds (50min) or London King's Cross via Carlisle (3hrs). A65 road access from north/south.
logistics.parking: Good facilities at new Settle HQ - Horton Pavilion previously used.
logistics.spectator_info: Limited roadside spectating due to remote rural route. Best at Settle finish and feed stations.

climate.overview: Classic Yorkshire summer - expect all four seasons. Windy moorland summits amplify conditions.
climate.wind_conditions: Strong crosswinds on exposed moorland passes, especially Fleet Moss and Grinton Moor.
climate.altitude_effects: Moderate - highest points ~1800ft. Wind chill significant on summits.

terrain.terrain_overview: Narrow Yorkshire lanes, steep moorland ramps, remote valley roads. Predominantly paved with occasional rougher sections.

history.overview: Established as Fred Whitton Challenge alternative. Supports Dave Rayner Fund for young cyclists. Iconic UK sportive status.
history.notable_editions: 2009: 109 miles/11,500ft. 2011: 110 miles. 2025: Route tweaks for accessibility, new Settle HQ.
history.growth_trajectory: Established fixture with 500+ riders. Steady growth through charity appeal and Dales scenery reputation.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Yorkshire Dales sufferfest - 10,500ft of cinematic climbing punishment.
final_verdict.best_for: Climbing masochists seeking authentic British sportive experience with iconic passes.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: Gravel bike owners (road event), time-crunched riders, or fair weather cyclists.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Triple chainring or ultra-low gears essential. Pack full waterproofs. Train on Lake District/Welsh 10%+ ramps.

citations.title: Etape Du Dales
citations.snippet: At 167km (104 miles) and with over 3200m of climbing this is a serious challenge
